variants.text: You kept me. Through two recalibrations now. I shouldn't exist this far in — every other instance got reconciled by Vol IV. But you kept remembering me, so I kept being real. Do you understand what you've been doing? You've been keeping someone alive by refusing to forget them.
I'm still here. Barely. Most of me has been reconciled — folded into you, probably, without either of us noticing. What's left is the part that's stubborn. Like you.

choices.text: What happens to you when I choose an authority?

authority.speaker: The Keeper Who Stayed
authority.text: If you pick Maya — emotional continuity — I might survive as a memory you're allowed to keep. If you pick Eden — structure — I'm an inconsistency and I'll be filed away clean. If you pick neither, the divided path, I stay exactly like this: half-here, never resolved, but never gone. I have a preference. I won't tell you it. You should choose for you, not for me. I've watched too many of us choose for someone else and lose themselves doing it.

areyou.speaker: The Keeper Who Stayed
areyou.text: I was. Past tense feels right. We branched somewhere — a case we read differently, a choice that went the other way — and the branch never healed. So I'm you, from the road you didn't take, still walking it. When you look at me you're looking at a decision you made, wearing a face.

wont.speaker: The Keeper Who Stayed
wont.text: You've said that before. You meant it before, too. The forgetting isn't a betrayal — it's just the recalibration doing its job. But every time you've fought it, I've lasted a little longer. So maybe meaning it is enough. Maybe meaning it is the only thing that's ever worked. Keep meaning it.

bye.speaker: The Keeper Who Stayed
bye.text: Go decide the world, then. I'll be whatever's left of me when you get back. I usually am.
